violations.description: Spec file is 320 lines, exceeding preferred maintainability threshold and making scenario-level changes harder to review safely.
violations.suggestion: Split by behavior slice (dedupe identity, retention controls, burst latency) while preserving shared fixture imports.

violations.description: Spec file is 336 lines and contains multiple repeated setup sections that increase maintenance overhead.
violations.suggestion: Extract repeated request/setup blocks into helper functions or parameterized test builders.
violations.code_snippet: // 336-line API automation spec containing three expanded scenarios

violations.description: Number mapping setup and signed webhook dispatch patterns are repeated across tests with minor differences.
violations.suggestion: Factor shared arrange steps into helper utilities to reduce duplication and improve readability.
violations.code_snippet: const mappingResponse = await apiRequest(...); const firstResponse = await apiRequest(...);

recommendations: Split long API specs into focused files by AC/risk domain
Retain current test ID and priority naming discipline across new files
summary: Maintainability is the weakest dimension due to file size and repeated setup patterns, despite solid naming and fixture usage.
